avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in all aspects. Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northeasterly wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed by the evening in particular in places that are protected from the wind. High-altitude starting zones have been replenished further quickly. In all aspects medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. In particular on wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The old snowpack is moist. The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The solar radiation will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some cases at low altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger remains significant, as drifts of wind-blown snow have formed in many places.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes storm force wind from northerly directions, wind slabs formed in the last few days in all aspects. They are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Also transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are especially precarious. Wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised as well in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. The wind has extensively reshaped the snowpack, causing the recent surface snow to be blown away and creating extensive eroded areas interspersed with areas of accumulation; in many areas, there are hard, icy surface crusts, some of which are hidden. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase in the afternoon.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in all aspects. The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. The wind will be strong.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 5 to 15 cm of snow will fall from midday above approximately 1600 m. The wind will be strong. As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form also in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. In some places the wind slabs will increase in size moderately as the day progresses. Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. They will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise.
The more recent wind slabs can be released, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm sign.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 2 to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
20 to 30 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m. The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, especially in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack. On southeast and southwest facing slopes below approximately 2400 m the snow is moist.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The wind will be strong. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in some places avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 5 cm of snow will fall. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of the northeasterly wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a freshening wind from northeasterly directions, further wind slabs will form in the course of the day. These can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

In particular on steep slopes the avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach large size in isolated cases.

Several mostly small natural dry avalanches are possible on very steep slopes.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Backcountry touring calls for careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 10 to 15 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1600 m.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northeasterly directions, further wind slabs will form. High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Over a wide area various wind slab layers are lying on a hard crust.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs are to be assessed with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The hard wind slabs are lying on a crust in particular on sunny slopes and at high altitude.
These can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes. Along the border with France the likelihood of avalanches is higher.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area wind slabs are lying on a hard crust, especially on sunny slopes in all altitude zones, and adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas at elevated altitudes.

High altitudes shady slopes: Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted.
Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes.

On sunnier slopes where temperatures are less cold, the hard surface of the snowpack will gradually soften over the course of the day.

In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, extensive wind slabs formed in all aspects. These can be released even by a single winter sport participant above approximately 2200 m, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: 10 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: In some localities 5 to 10 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m. As a consequence of new snow and wind from northeasterly directions, rather small wind slabs formed. These can in some places be released, in particular by large loads. In particular on wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 5 to 10 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Over a wide area various wind slab layers are lying on a hard crust.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, will fall from midday above approximately 1600 m. The wind will be strong. In some places the wind slabs will increase in size moderately as the day progresses. These can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 2 to 5 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell on Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, especially in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack. On southeast and southwest facing slopes below approximately 2400 m the snow is moist.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The wind will be strong. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1400 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind was storm force in some cases. The northwesterly wind has transported some snow. Avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment: In particular on steep slopes small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment: The solar radiation will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in all aspects. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes. The surface of the snowpack will soften quickly.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can in some cases be released easily.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
